concerns.text: Constitution Principle II requires Reference-Validator citation verification BEFORE analysis begins, but T049-T051 are scheduled in Phase 6 (after US1 data ingestion and US2 analysis). Plan.md explicitly states this is a 'blocking gate' that must complete before remaining phases. This is a producer-consumer ordering violation: citations must be validated before they can be used in analysis.

T010 (integration test for data ingestion pipeline) is listed BEFORE T017 (create sample dataset for testing). The integration test requires sample data to validate against. This violates producer-before-consumer ordering. T017 should precede T010.

T015 (logging for ingestion/validation) is placed after core validation functions (T012-T014). Logging infrastructure should be established early in the phase, not after implementation. Consider moving T015 before T011a.

T030 (logging for analysis operations) is placed at the end of US2 implementation, after all statistical functions. Logging should be established before implementing dependent functions. Consider moving T030 before T021a.
T039 (logging for visualization/export) is placed at the end of US3. Same pattern as T015/T030 - logging infrastructure should precede implementation tasks. Consider moving T039 before T033a.

T043 (create data-model.md) is placed AFTER schema contracts (T004-T006). The data-model.md documents the entities that the schemas are based on. Documentation should precede or accompany the artifacts it describes. Consider moving T043 before T004.

T046a-c (unit tests for edge cases: missing data, skewed distributions, collinearity) are deferred to Phase 7 (Polish). These test edge cases that are core to US1 (missing data) and US2 (skewed distributions, collinearity). They should be part of their respective user story implementation, not deferred to polish.
